chunks.text: Physical Intelligence, an artificial intelligence startup co-founded by EECS Associate Professor Sergey Levine, announced it had raised $400 million in financing from major investors. The company aims to develop foundational software designed to be compatible with any robot, moving away from the traditional method of creating software tailored to specific robots.

Hany Farid, a professor of digital forensics at the University of California, Berkeley, identified evidence of AI manipulation in a handful of recent Storm-1516 videos, including one from July of a man posing as a luxury car salesman to falsely claim to have sold Zelenska a car.

Letta, founded by Berkeley PhD students Sarah Wooders and Charles Packer, is a highly anticipated AI startup launch. It is a child of Berkeley’s Sky Computing Lab and is the commercial entity of the popular MemGPT open source project.

Building safe and capable models is one of the greatest challenges of our time. Join Professor Hannah Fry as she delves into critical questions with EECS Associate Professor Anca Dragan, lead researcher on AI safety and alignment.

Opaque Launches New Platform For Running AI Workloads on Encrypted Data. The Confidential AI platform was developed at the Berkeley RISELab, known for technologies like Apache Spark and Databricks, and is based on the MC2 platform created there.

Students express concern that generative AI will affect the software engineering job market, especially for entry-level positions, but they are still excited about careers in computing, according to John DeNero in an email to Business Insider.

POLITICO interviewed EECS Professor Ben Recht as part of their Future in Five Questions series. CS Professor Ben Recht was also quoted in POLITICO in response to an AI app that creates customized music.

An OpenAI spinoff has built an AI model that helps robots learn tasks like humans. The New York Times profiles Covariant, an Emeryville-based AI and robotics company founded by CS Professor Pieter Abbeel and his former students.
